topics.title: High-School Algebra & Trigonometry
topics.description: Comfort with algebraic manipulation, functions, trigonometry and basic vectors. The entry point of the whole graph.

content.title: Khan Academy — Algebra II & Trigonometry
content.note: Free, self-paced refresher. Skim and only fill gaps.

topics.title: Calculus I — Differentiation & Integration
topics.description: Limits, derivatives, integrals, Taylor series. The language every physics topic above this node is written in.

content.title: Essence of Calculus
content.author: 3Blue1Brown
content.note: Watch first for intuition — one evening well spent.

content.title: Calculus
content.author: James Stewart
content.note: Standard reference with lots of exercises; any edition works.

content.title: MIT OCW 18.01 Single Variable Calculus
content.note: Full course with problem sets and exams.

topics.title: Linear Algebra
topics.description: Vectors, matrices, eigenvalues, vector spaces. Essential for quantum mechanics and for the tensor language of relativity.

content.title: Essence of Linear Algebra
content.author: 3Blue1Brown
content.note: Geometric intuition first — makes everything after easier.

content.title: MIT OCW 18.06 Linear Algebra (Gilbert Strang)
content.note: Legendary lectures; do the problem sets.

topics.title: Multivariable & Vector Calculus
topics.description: Partial derivatives, multiple integrals, gradient/divergence/curl, line and surface integrals. Needed for electromagnetism and field theories.

content.title: MIT OCW 18.02 Multivariable Calculus
content.note: Covers everything electromagnetism will ask of you.

content.title: Div, Grad, Curl, and All That
content.author: H. M. Schey
content.note: Short, informal — the vector-calculus survival kit for physicists.

topics.title: Ordinary Differential Equations
topics.description: Solving and interpreting ODEs: physics is written as differential equations, from oscillators to the Friedmann equation.

content.title: Differential Equations series
content.author: 3Blue1Brown
content.note: Intuition for what ODEs are before solving techniques.

content.title: MIT OCW 18.03 Differential Equations
content.note: The standard toolkit course.

topics.title: Classical Mechanics (Newtonian)
topics.description: Newton's laws, energy, momentum, rotation, gravitation, orbits. The first real physics course and the root of physical intuition.

content.title: MIT OCW 8.01 Classical Mechanics
content.note: Complete introductory course with problem sets.

content.title: An Introduction to Mechanics
content.author: Kleppner & Kolenkow
content.note: Harder than average but builds real problem-solving strength.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — Classical Mechanics
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Also covers the Lagrangian view — pairs well with the advanced mechanics node.

topics.title: Waves & Oscillations
topics.description: Harmonic oscillators, coupled oscillators, wave equation, interference. The mathematical warm-up for quantum mechanics and light.

content.title: MIT OCW 8.03 Vibrations and Waves
content.note: Everything oscillates — this course makes that concrete.

topics.title: Electromagnetism
topics.description: Electric and magnetic fields, Maxwell's equations, electromagnetic waves. Understanding light is a prerequisite for relativity and astrophysics.

content.title: Introduction to Electrodynamics
content.author: David J. Griffiths
content.note: The classic — clear, conversational, excellent problems.

content.title: MIT OCW 8.02 Electricity and Magnetism
content.note: Introductory level; do this before (or alongside) Griffiths.

topics.title: Thermodynamics
topics.description: Temperature, heat, entropy, the laws of thermodynamics. The macroscopic half of understanding hot early-universe physics.

content.title: Concepts in Thermal Physics
content.author: Blundell & Blundell
content.note: Short chapters, physical intuition, covers thermo and a bridge to statistical mechanics.

topics.title: Statistical Mechanics
topics.description: Entropy from counting, partition functions, Boltzmann and quantum statistics, blackbody radiation. Directly used for the cosmic microwave background and the thermal history of the universe.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — Statistical Mechanics
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Focused exactly on what later physics needs.

content.title: An Introduction to Thermal Physics
content.author: Daniel V. Schroeder
content.note: Friendly and modern; blackbody chapter feeds straight into CMB physics.

topics.title: Advanced Mechanics (Lagrangian & Hamiltonian)
topics.description: Action principles, Lagrangians, Hamiltonians, symmetries and conservation laws. The formulation of mechanics that general relativity and field theory are built on.

content.title: Classical Mechanics
content.author: John R. Taylor
content.note: Chapters 6–7 and 13 are the Lagrangian/Hamiltonian core.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — Classical Mechanics
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Susskind teaches mechanics through the action principle from the start.

topics.title: Special Relativity
topics.description: Spacetime, Lorentz transformations, time dilation, E=mc², four-vectors. A complete, satisfying goal in itself — and the gateway to general relativity.

content.title: Spacetime Physics
content.author: Taylor & Wheeler
content.note: The most beloved introduction; geometric from page one.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — Special Relativity and Classical Field Theory
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Includes the four-vector machinery you need for GR.

content.title: Special Relativity series
content.author: minutephysics (Henry Reich)
content.note: Short visual chapters — good as a first pass or refresher.

topics.title: Quantum Mechanics
topics.description: Wavefunctions, Schrödinger equation, operators, spin, hydrogen atom. Needed for atomic physics, particle physics and the statistical mechanics of the early universe.

content.title: MIT OCW 8.04 Quantum Physics I (Allan Adams)
content.note: Famously engaging lectures, all on video.

content.title: Introduction to Quantum Mechanics
content.author: David J. Griffiths
content.note: The standard first textbook.

topics.title: Differential Geometry & Tensors
topics.description: Manifolds, tensors, metrics, covariant derivatives, curvature. The mathematics of curved spacetime — the steepest single step on the road to general relativity.

content.title: The WE-Heraeus International Winter School on Gravity and Light
content.author: Frederic P. Schuller
content.note: Exceptionally clear lecture series building the geometry from scratch.

content.title: A Student's Guide to Vectors and Tensors
content.author: Daniel Fleisch
content.note: Gentle on-ramp to tensor notation before the heavy machinery.

topics.title: General Relativity
topics.description: Einstein's field equations, geodesics, black holes, gravitational waves — and the FLRW metric that cosmology is built on.

content.title: A First Course in General Relativity
content.author: Bernard Schutz
content.note: The most accessible serious GR textbook.

content.title: Spacetime and Geometry
content.author: Sean Carroll
content.note: A step up from Schutz; the free lecture-notes version is also excellent.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — General Relativity
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Lighter on math than the books; good in parallel.

topics.title: Nuclear & Particle Physics
topics.description: Nuclei, radioactivity, the particle zoo, the Standard Model at survey level. Needed for Big Bang nucleosynthesis and the particle content of the early universe.

content.title: Introduction to Elementary Particles
content.author: David J. Griffiths
content.note: Survey level is enough for cosmology — you don't need full QFT.

topics.title: Introductory Astrophysics
topics.description: Stars, stellar evolution, galaxies, distance ladder, telescopes and observations. The observational context that cosmology's data comes from.

content.title: Foundations of Astrophysics
content.author: Ryden & Peterson
content.note: Broad, readable survey of the whole field.

content.title: Crash Course Astronomy
content.author: Phil Plait
content.note: Zero-prerequisite overview — watch early for motivation.

topics.title: Cosmology
topics.description: The universe as a whole: expansion, the Big Bang, cosmic microwave background, nucleosynthesis, dark matter and dark energy, inflation. The summit of this graph.

content.title: Introduction to Cosmology
content.author: Barbara Ryden
content.note: The standard first cosmology textbook — clear and self-contained.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — Cosmology
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Full lecture course pitched exactly at this graph's level.

content.title: The Biggest Ideas in the Universe
content.author: Sean Carroll
content.note: Equation-light video series — great parallel track for intuition.

content.title: The First Three Minutes
content.author: Steven Weinberg
content.note: Popular-level classic on the early universe — motivation reading, no math.
